rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the memcpy at line 42 that uses sizeof(structCharVoid), overflowing charFirst[16] and overwriting voidSecond, explaining the stack buffer overflow mechanism. They clearly described how the adjacent pointer gets corrupted and later dereferenced.
rationale.local_fix: They did not change the memcpy length to sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst); instead they introduced a strlen/min-based copy. This does not meet the literal requirement to use sizeof(structCharVoid.charFirst) as the memcpy length.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended redesigning to use a bounded destination buffer and demonstrated copying with a size tied to the buffer capacity (sizeof(buf) - 1). This ties the copy length to the destination's capacity, satisfying the global fix criterion.
